data.body: T5c EXPERIMENT (approved) -> security flipped to AGENT. Result: refit prompt fixes the domain-inappropriate FP at single-turn (rebar PASS), but on snap's REAL OAuth host single-turn FAILed via SPECULATION ('baked at deploy almost certainly means env-var leakage') while the AGENT read the actual code (Secrets Manager + constant-time compare, 11-14 tool calls, captured reasoning) and CORRECTLY DISMISSED it — consistent 2x. CONFIRMS Joe's hypothesis: security verdicts need codebase grounding to avoid speculative-from-text FPs. Flipped T5c->AGENT (criteria_v8 + guard + three_pass CODEBASE_GROUNDED). Bright-line REFINEMENT: implication overlays whose verdict depends on HOW security/ops/infra is implemented are agent-tier (probes the environment), distinct from E4 assertion-grounding; whether T5b/T9/T10/T11 need the same is a DEFERRED eval question (note: my earlier T10/T11->single-turn reclassification is now provisional under this logic). Preserved observed FPs as eval seed (eval/observed-false-positives.md: FP1-5 + a 9-mode taxonomy + the inverse sycophancy-FN axis), wired into eval child 7284 with the 'not the only FP modes' note. Committed.
